message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
This is a dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (do NOT flag as bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only, nothing leaves the box), no metered API (subscription OAuth only), engine never imports adapters/datasets (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends are lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; prefer findings you can back with a concrete repro/PoC against the venv. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects (not style/nits). Each finding needs a real file path and, where possible, a line.

You are independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A prior adversarial assessor reported this production-readiness finding:

TITLE: GateReview is a frozen dataclass whose Mapping payload is not frozen — mutable-in-place and unhashable (issue #7 escape)
DIMENSION: correctness & concurrency
CLAIMED SEVERITY: MEDIUM
FILE: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py 72-80
DESCRIPTION: GateReview is declared @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) with a `payload: Mapping[str, Any]` field but, unlike EVERY other frozen mapping-bearing value record in the engine (ProvenanceEntry, GateDecision, Corpus, ClaimRecord, Verdict, Hypothesis, BaselineArtifact, ReasoningRequest, etc.), it has no __post_init__ that normalizes the field through freeze_mapping. This reproduces both halves of the codebase's own documented issue #7 (see src/assay_engine/_frozen.py module docstring):

1. Mutable-in-place: `frozen=True` only blocks attribute rebinding; the contained dict stays live, so a 'frozen' GateReview can be silently altered after construction AND it aliases whatever dict was passed in. PoC against the venv: `gr = GateReview(...payload=shared); gr.payload['k']='MUTATED'` succeeds and `shared['k']` becomes 'MUTATED'.
2. Unhashable: `frozen=True` auto-generates __hash__ over all fields, so the instance *looks* hashable but `hash(gr)` raises `TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict'` (verified in venv). It cannot be put in a set/dict or used as a cache key despite being frozen.

GateReview is the packet handed to the caller-supplied `gate_handler` (a trust boundary the surrounding code otherwise treats defensively, e.g. provenance.as_recorder reads gate decisions defensively against 'booby-trapped' objects). A handler can mutate review.payload in place. The two in-tree construction sites (pipeline.py:394, :440) pass fresh dict literals, so there is no leak back to an external caller *today*, but the immutability/hashability guarantee the `frozen=True` annotation advertises is false.
SUGGESTED FIX: Add the same __post_init__ used by sibling records, e.g. in pipeline.py:

    from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping  # add to imports

    @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
    class GateReview:
        ...
        payload: Mapping[str, Any]
        def __post_init__(self) -> None:
            object.__setattr__(self, 'payload', freeze_mapping(self.payload))

This makes payload deep-immutable and renders GateReview hashable, matching the engine-wide invariant.

Independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the actual code; attempt a concrete PoC against the venv if applicable. Set is_real=false if it is a false positive, already-handled, or merely a documented/justified design choice given the hard constraints. Recalibrate severity. Provide your evidence (what you read/ran).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import datetime as _dt
32	from dataclasses import dataclass
33	from pathlib import Path
34	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping
35	
36	from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint
37	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
38	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord
39	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureMatrix
40	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
41	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
42	from assay_engine.methodology.adjudication import (
43	    ClaimConfirmer,
44	    SourceScorecard,
45	    adjudicate_with_baseline,
46	)
47	from assay_engine.methodology.discovery import HeldOutConfirmer, subset_corpus
48	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import (
49	    ClaimBlindGuard,
50	    DiscoverConfirmSplit,
51	    FirewallViolation,
52	)
53	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
54	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import TimestampAuthority, require_preregistered
55	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
56	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import OtelTracer, Tracer
57	from assay_engine.observability.tracking import ExperimentTracker
58	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateDecision, GateError
59	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase, required_phases
60	from assay_engine.persistence.versioning import DataVersioner
61	from assay_engine.provenance import Clock, ProvenanceEntry, ProvenanceTrail
62	
63	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
64	
65	DiscoverFn = Callable[[Corpus], Iterable[Hypothesis]]
66	
67	
68	class IngestionError(ValueError):
69	    """Ingestion failed (the parser raised or produced an unusable corpus)."""
70	
71	
72	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
73	class GateReview:
74	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition."""
75	
76	    gate: str
77	    frm: Phase
78	    to: Phase
79	    summary: str
80	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
81	
82	
83	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
84	
85	
86	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
87	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
88	
89	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
90	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
91	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
92	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
93	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
94	    """
95	    return GateDecision(
96	        approved=True,
97	        gate=review.gate,
98	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
99	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
100	    )

1	"""Deep-immutable, hashable mapping for the engine's frozen value records.
2	
3	Many engine dataclasses are ``frozen=True`` and carry mapping fields (``evidence``,
4	``metadata``, ``attributes``, ``params``, ``assertion``, ``provenance``, ``contents``,
5	``determinism``). With a plain ``dict`` default two problems arise (audit pass 1, issue #7):
6	
7	1. ``frozen=True`` auto-generates ``__hash__`` over all fields, so the instances *look*
8	   hashable but raise ``TypeError`` the moment they are hashed (a dict is unhashable).
9	2. ``frozen`` only blocks attribute rebinding; the contained dict stays mutable in place,
10	   so a "frozen" provenance record can be silently altered after construction.
11	
12	:class:`FrozenDict` fixes both: it is an immutable :class:`~collections.abc.Mapping` and is
13	hashable. :func:`freeze` recursively converts nested mappings/sequences/sets so the whole
14	structure is immutable and hashable. Each affected dataclass normalizes its mapping fields
15	through :func:`freeze` in ``__post_init__`` (via ``object.__setattr__`` to satisfy frozen).
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from collections.abc import Mapping
21	from typing import Any, Iterator
22	
23	
24	class FrozenDict(Mapping[str, Any]):
25	    """An immutable, hashable mapping. Item assignment is impossible; hashing is stable."""
26	
27	    __slots__ = ("_data", "_hash")
28	
29	    def __init__(self, data: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None) -> None:
30	        # Freeze values so a directly-constructed FrozenDict satisfies its own
31	        # immutable+hashable contract even from nested-mutable input (audit pass 3, issue #27).
32	        # freeze() is idempotent, so routing through freeze_mapping costs only one extra pass.
33	        self._data: dict[str, Any] = (
34	            {k: freeze(v) for k, v in data.items()} if data is not None else {}
35	        )
36	        self._hash: int | None = None
37	
38	    def __getitem__(self, key: str) -> Any:
39	        return self._data[key]
40	
41	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[str]:
42	        return iter(self._data)
43	
44	    def __len__(self) -> int:
45	        return len(self._data)
46	
47	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
48	        return f"FrozenDict({self._data!r})"
49	
50	    def __hash__(self) -> int:
51	        if self._hash is None:
52	            self._hash = hash(frozenset(self._data.items()))
53	        return self._hash
54	
55	    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:
56	        if isinstance(other, Mapping):
57	            return dict(self._data) == dict(other)
58	        return NotImplemented
59	
60	
61	def freeze(value: Any) -> Any:
62	    """Recursively convert ``value`` into an immutable, hashable form.
63	
64	    Mappings → :class:`FrozenDict`, lists/tuples → tuples, sets → frozensets. Known mutable
65	    buffers are converted to hashable equivalents (``bytearray`` → ``bytes``;
66	    ``array.array`` / numpy-like arrays exposing ``tobytes`` → a ``(kind, shape, bytes)``
67	    tuple). Any remaining leaf that is not hashable raises ``TypeError`` *here, at
68	    construction time* — rather than silently passing through and resurfacing the error the
69	    moment the record is hashed (audit pass 2, issue #19; the incomplete half of #7).
70	    """
71	    if isinstance(value, FrozenDict):
72	        return value
73	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
74	        return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
75	    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
76	        return tuple(freeze(v) for v in value)
77	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
78	        return frozenset(freeze(v) for v in value)
79	    if isinstance(value, bytearray):
80	        return bytes(value)
81	    # Duck-typed buffer/array support (e.g. array.array, numpy.ndarray) without importing
82	    # numpy: an array-like exposing tobytes() is canonicalized to a hashable descriptor tuple.
83	    # Require an array-ish marker (shape/typecode/dtype) so an unrelated object that merely
84	    # happens to have a tobytes() is left to the hashability check below (fix-review nit, #19).
85	    tobytes = getattr(value, "tobytes", None)
86	    array_like = hasattr(value, "shape") or hasattr(value, "typecode") or hasattr(value, "dtype")
87	    if callable(tobytes) and array_like and not isinstance(value, (bytes, str)):
88	        shape = getattr(value, "shape", None)
89	        kind = getattr(getattr(value, "dtype", None), "str", None) or getattr(
90	            value, "typecode", type(value).__name__
91	        )
92	        # Route the descriptor through freeze() so a malformed tobytes() returning a
93	        # non-hashable value is still caught loud at construction (audit pass 3, issue #26).
94	        return freeze((str(kind), tuple(shape) if shape is not None else None, tobytes()))
95	    try:
96	        hash(value)
97	    except TypeError as exc:
98	        raise TypeError(
99	            f"freeze: leaf of type {type(value).__name__!r} is not hashable and has no "
100	            "known immutable conversion; a frozen value record cannot contain it"
101	        ) from exc
102	    return value
103	
104	
105	def freeze_mapping(value: Mapping[str, Any]) -> FrozenDict:
106	    """Normalize a mapping field to a deep-immutable :class:`FrozenDict`."""
107	    return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})
108

content.text: The claim is confirmed by reading the code: `GateReview` has no `__post_init__` while siblings do. Let me verify the sibling claim and run a PoC.

content.text: Confirmed: every sibling frozen record normalizes its mapping field via `freeze_mapping` in `__post_init__`, while `GateReview` does not. Now let me run the PoC against the venv.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "
from assay_engine.pipeline import GateReview
from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase

shared = {'k': 'orig'}
gr = GateReview(gate='g', frm=Phase.PREREGISTER, to=Phase.CONFIRM, summary='s', payload=shared)

# 1. mutable-in-place + aliasing
gr.payload['k'] = 'MUTATED'
print('mutated payload:', gr.payload['k'])
print('shared aliased:', shared['k'])

# 2. hashability
try:
    h = hash(gr)
    print('hash ok:', h)
except TypeError as e:
    print('hash FAILED:', e)

# contrast with sibling GateDecision
from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateDecision
gd = GateDecision(approved=True, gate='g', reason='r', evidence={'x':'y'})
try:
    gd.evidence['x']='Z'
    print('GateDecision evidence mutated:', gd.evidence['x'])
except TypeError as e:
    print('GateDecision evidence immutable:', e)
print('GateDecision hashable:', hash(gd) is not None)
"
input.description: PoC for mutability and hashability of GateReview

content.content:
mutated payload: MUTATED
shared aliased: MUTATED
hash FAILED: unhashable type: 'dict'
GateDecision evidence immutable: 'FrozenDict' object does not support item assignment
GateDecision hashable: True

content.text:
Both halves reproduced. The PoC confirms:
1. `GateReview.payload` is mutable in place and aliases the passed dict.
2. `hash(gr)` raises `TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict'` despite `frozen=True`.
3. The sibling `GateDecision` correctly blocks mutation and is hashable.

Now let me assess real-world impact: are the in-tree construction sites the only ones, and does any handler/code path hash or rely on immutability?

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && grep -rn "GateReview\|\.payload\b" src/ tests/ | grep -iv "ProvenanceEntry\|trail\|provenance.py" | grep -i "gatereview\|review.payload\|gr.payload"
input.description: Find all GateReview usages and payload accesses

content.content:
src/assay_engine/__init__.py:46:    GateReview,
src/assay_engine/__init__.py:81:    "GateReview",
src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:73:class GateReview:
src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:83:GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:86:def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:232:    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:394:                    GateReview(
src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:440:                GateReview(
src/assay_engine/orchestration/gates.py:17:  ``GateReview``/``GateHandler`` model for in-process governance (a handler may approve, block,
tests/test_pipeline.py:182:        seen["ids"] = list(review.payload["claim_ids"])
tests/test_pipeline.py:230:        seen["ids"] = list(review.payload["hypothesis_ids"])

content.text:
`GateReview` is exported in the public API (`__init__.py`), so external/operator-supplied gate handlers receive it. The two in-tree sites pass fresh dict literals (no leak today), and `auto_approve` doesn't hash. So no active bug today, but the `frozen=True` contract is genuinely false, and as a public-API value record handed across a trust boundary, the invariant is meaningful.

This is a real correctness defect: a one-line-per-record invariant that the entire engine documents (issue #7) and applies to every sibling, missed only here. Impact is latent (no current data corruption since callers pass literals), and the fix is the engine's own established pattern. MEDIUM is slightly high for something with no live failure path today; LOW is more accurate, but given it's public-API + trust boundary + the engine's own documented invariant being violated, I'll keep it at LOW-to-MEDIUM. I'll call it LOW given there's no concrete failure path in current code, but it's a legitimate consistency/contract defect worth fixing.

input.reasoning:
The finding is technically accurate and reproduces against the venv. GateReview (src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:72-80) is declared @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) with a payload: Mapping[str, Any] field but lacks the __post_init__ that normalizes the field through freeze_mapping. I verified that EVERY sibling frozen mapping-bearing record does this normalization: GateDecision (gates.py:48-49), ProvenanceEntry (provenance.py:64-65), Corpus/schema records (schema.py:44,62,77+89), ClaimRecord (claims.py:37-39), Hypothesis (hypothesis.py:80-83), Verdict (verdict.py:39-40), BaselineArtifact (toolkit.py:38-40). GateReview is the sole omission, and the codebase explicitly documents this exact two-part hazard as issue #7 in _frozen.py's module docstring. My PoC confirmed both halves: (1) gr.payload['k']='MUTATED' succeeds on a 'frozen' instance and aliases the input dict, and (2) hash(gr) raises TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict' — while the sibling GateDecision correctly rejects mutation (FrozenDict has no item assignment) and is hashable.

However, severity should be recalibrated DOWN from MEDIUM to LOW. There is no live failure path in the current code: the only two construction sites (pipeline.py:394 and :440) pass fresh dict literals, so no external dict is aliased, and nothing in the engine hashes a GateReview or relies on its immutability — the gate handlers (auto_approve, the tests at test_pipeline.py:182/230) only read review.payload. So today there is no data-corruption or crash path. The residual issue is a false advertisement of the frozen=True/hashable contract on a public-API value record (exported via __init__.py) handed across the gate-handler trust boundary — a real consistency/contract defect, but latent. The suggested fix is exactly the engine's own established one-line pattern and is correct.
input.evidence: Read src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:72-80 (GateReview has no __post_init__) and construction sites :394, :440 (fresh dict literals). Read src/assay_engine/_frozen.py (documents issue #7: frozen-but-mutable + looks-hashable-but-raises; freeze_mapping is the normalizer). grep confirmed all sibling records use __post_init__ + freeze_mapping (gates.py:48-49, provenance.py:64-65, schema.py:44/62/77/89, claims.py:37-39, hypothesis.py:80-83, verdict.py:39-40, toolkit.py:38-40). Ran PoC in .venv: 'gr.payload[k]=MUTATED' succeeded, shared input dict aliased to 'MUTATED', hash(gr) raised TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict'; contrast GateDecision rejected mutation ('FrozenDict object does not support item assignment') and hashed fine. grep for GateReview/payload usage shows only literal-dict constructions and read-only handler access (test_pipeline.py:182,230) — no live hash or external-dict path, hence LOW not MEDIUM. GateReview is exported in src/assay_engine/__init__.py:46/81 (public API across the gate-handler trust boundary).

content.text:
Verified. The finding is real but over-rated at MEDIUM.

Summary: `GateReview` in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:72-80` is the only frozen mapping-bearing value record in the engine missing the `__post_init__`/`freeze_mapping` normalization that every sibling applies (confirmed across gates.py, provenance.py, schema.py, claims.py, hypothesis.py, verdict.py, toolkit.py). I reproduced both halves of the codebase's own documented issue #7 against the venv: the "frozen" payload is mutable in place and aliases the input dict, and `hash(gr)` raises `TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict'`.

I recalibrated severity from MEDIUM to LOW because there is no live failure path today — the only two construction sites pass fresh dict literals and no code hashes or relies on the immutability of a `GateReview`. It is a genuine but latent contract/consistency defect on a public-API record crossing the gate-handler trust boundary; the suggested one-line fix matches the engine's established pattern.
